preview_text:
The model favours England at 53%, with Croatia positioned as underdog at 18%. The gap between the two teams is substantial, reflecting a moderate favourite-versus-challenger dynamic rather than a close contest.

The W/D/L spread suggests England's advantage is real but not overwhelming. A draw is plausible at 29% of outcomes, narrowing the gap between England victory and a stalemate. The match carries competitive texture: Croatia holds enough probability mass to trouble the favourite, yet England enters with a clear edge in the forecast distribution.

The model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. Sportsbooks price England slightly higher at 56% versus the model's 53%, a difference of roughly 3 percentage points, while both sources converge on draw probability near 26–29%. Polymarket prices track similarly to the consensus. These minor variations fall within typical market noise and suggest broad agreement on the match structure.
